title: salix purpurea subsp. eburnea
intro: Salix purpurea subsp. eburnea, commonly known as silver willow or eburnean willow, is a subspecies of willow tree that is native to Europe and Asia. The bark of this plant contains salicylic acid, a compound similar to aspirin, which makes it valuable for its medicinal properties. The health benefits of Salix purpurea subsp. eburnea include its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can be used to treat conditions such as arthritis and migraines. Salix purpurea subsp. eburnea can be prepared for herbal use through infusions, decoctions, and salves, which can be applied topically or ingested to harness its medicinal properties.
